reddit_title: Spinal cord injury from a car accident in El Paso, TX — what legal steps should the family be taking right now?
reddit_body:
If someone in your family has suffered a spinal cord injury in a vehicle crash in El Paso, the legal clock starts ticking almost immediately. Texas has a two-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but the real urgency is evidence preservation — accident scene data, witness statements, vehicle black box information, and medical records all need to be secured as soon as possible.

One of the most important early steps is having an attorney who handles catastrophic injury cases evaluate the claim. These cases are different from typical fender-benders — they involve life-care planners, medical experts, vocational economists, and long-term damage calculations that general practitioners may not be equipped to handle. Make sure whoever you hire has specific experience with serious and catastrophic injury claims in Texas.

For families trying to find qualified representation quickly, services like wreckmatch.com can match you with attorneys experienced in severe injury cases without having to cold-call firms during an already stressful time. Whatever path you take, act quickly, document everything, and don't give a recorded statement to any insurance company before speaking with an attorney.
